title: rig.yaml — rig-cli declarative config
description: The committed source of truth for a repo's setup (and ~/.config/rig/config.yaml). See docs/config-schema.md for the human-readable reference.

agent_tools_source.description: the agent-tools checkout to apply FROM (default: auto-detect)

stack.description: the repo's stack preset `l1/lang[/framework]` (e.g. mobile/swift/swiftui, frontend/ts/react, backend/python). l1 in mobile|frontend|backend|desktop|embedded|system; lang required; framework optional. Selects by-stack skills. Global default is optional; a per-repo value is expected (soft-required).

defaults.description: cross-category fallback targets + the on-conflict policy.

on_conflict.description: what apply does when a target already exists

scripts.description: project-local named commands consumed by dev helpers; rig accepts and preserves the map.

dev.description: project-local dev/e2e lifecycle metadata consumed by dev helpers; rig accepts and preserves the map.

skills.description: advisory markdown rules copied into the agent skills dir (opt-out model).

harness_link.description: also symlink each installed skill into the harness discovery dir

universal.description: the universal skill set (opt-out via disable / opt-in via enable).

by_type.description: by-type skill bundles for the detected project type.

enable.description: which by-type bundles to install whole

by_stack.description: by-stack skills auto-selected by the repo's declared `stack` (hierarchical prefix match); tune with disable / per-item overrides.

disable.description: by-stack skill item names (`by-stack/<l1>/<lang>[/<fw>]/<name>`) to drop even though the declared stack would select them

items.description: per-skill overrides keyed by `by-stack/<l1>/<lang>[/<fw>]/<name>` (`enabled: true` force-adds an off-stack skill; `enabled: false` drops one)

agent_hooks.description: programmatic guards that block before a side effect (no-verify, secrets, raw merge).

target_kind.description: legacy ignored scaffold key; accepted for old configs but not used for rendering

worktree_only.description: enforce the worktree-only workflow: the worktree-only-writes hook DENIES an Edit/Write while the checkout sits on the default branch (main/master). Opt-IN, default OFF — a repo that works directly on main (e.g. 3d-cli) leaves it off and is never blocked. Escape hatch: RIG_ALLOW_MAIN_EDIT=1. (Alex tg#5742.)

orchestrator_only.description: keep the orchestrator thin: the orchestrator-stays-thin hook blocks inline implementation Bash / code Edits by the main thread (delegate to a subagent). Opt-OUT, default ON — set false to exempt a repo that legitimately works inline (e.g. 3d-cli). Escape hatch: ALLOW_ORCHESTRATOR_WORK=1 + reason. (Alex tg#5743.)

items.description: per-hook overrides (enabled / on_error) keyed by hook name

git_hooks.description: the global-hook dispatcher: your hooks run in every repo, even ones that hijack core.hooksPath.

set_global_hooks_path.description: wire it as git's global core.hooksPath

fragments.description: per-fragment overrides keyed by fragment name (secret-scan, …)

ci.description: vendor-neutral CI gates (secret-scan, codeql, dependency-review, ship, …).

items.description: per-gate overrides (enabled / tier / variant / …) keyed by gate name

mcp.description: MCP registrations (review, code-search) — callable from any agent.

items.description: per-server overrides (command / args / env / server / enabled) keyed by server name

command.description: server executable, or legacy command string when args is omitted

mode.description: machine-wide operating mode; `autonomous` provisions review/fix, quorum, escalation, and parallelism policy.

autonomous.description: policy knobs active when mode.name is autonomous.

max_iterations.description: maximum review/fix iterations before escalation or budget stop

decisions.description: decision-making policy before asking the user.

min_models.description: minimum distinct models in the quorum

escalation.description: when and how an agent may escalate to the user.

framework_skill.description: skill that defines escalation message behavior

require_parallel_worktree_comparison.description: compare independent worktree attempts before escalation

candidates.description: number of independent candidate implementations to compare

development_tools.description: extra allowlisted development-tool permission flows for autonomous mode.

allow.description: raw permission allow rules to add while in autonomous mode

reserve_slots.description: slots held back for urgent work

harness.description: the agent harness's auto/permission mode — provisioned, not hand-toggled.

kind.description: which harness to provision (skills-dir: claude-code/codex; native-discovery: opencode; instruction-file: pi/commandcode; codex is also instruction-file via AGENTS.md). The auto/permission-MODE write is claude-code-only today; other kinds still get their skill discovery provisioned.

kinds.description: additional harnesses to provision alongside harness.kind. Use this when one machine runs multiple harnesses: the primary kind keeps its auto-mode/settings_path behavior, while additional kinds get skill discovery, agent-hook descriptors, and any supported hook bridge. If agent_hooks.target pins descriptors to one explicit target, supported bridges are registered with a descriptor-dir override.

auto_mode.description: true → auto-accept tool calls; false → interactive

self_merge.description: true → add the ship allow rules (Bash(gh ship:*), Bash(*/pr-ship.sh:*), Bash(*/ship.sh:*)) to permissions.allow so the auto-mode Bash gate stops vetoing gh ship, PLUS the natural-language carve-out to autoMode.allow (clears the Merge-Without-Review + Self-Approval soft blocks for the agent's OWN PRs); auto-mode only; the Bash(gh pr merge:*) deny and every other classifier rule (incl. the anti-exfil hard rule) stay

mode.description: pin the exact permission value (overrides auto_mode map)

settings_path.description: override the per-harness settings/config/plugin path; absent means rig uses the harness-specific default

hook_bridge.description: wire the agents-hooks/v1 dispatcher into the supported harness config.

enabled.description: wire the supported harness hook-bridge dispatcher

python.description: the interpreter the dispatcher runs under

permissions.description: reconcile the harness permissions layer: the command allowlist + the deny/ask rule baselines.

kind.description: which harness's permissions to provision; null/absent means fan out to supported harness.kind plus harness.kinds

tools.description: command names to pre-allow (replaces the default set)

extra.description: command names to ADD on top of the set

disable.description: command names to drop from rig's desired set

allow.description: raw permission-rule entries asserted present in the allow list, on TOP of the tool-derived ones

deny.description: permission-rule entries asserted present in the deny list (REPLACES the baked baseline)

ask.description: permission-rule entries asserted present in the ask list (REPLACES the baked baseline)

models.description: a daily cron that runs the model-freshness checker and proposes version bumps.

label.description: the launchd Label / crontab sentinel identity

agents_md.description: one canonical AGENTS.md + a CLAUDE.md symlink, so every harness reads the same instructions.

github.description: GitHub repository settings rig reconciles via gh api + agent-browser.

ruleset.description: a branch ruleset on the default branch (the modern branch-protection replacement).

required_conversation_resolution.description: require every review thread resolved before merge (required_review_thread_resolution)

dismiss_stale_reviews.description: dismiss stale approvals on a new push (dismiss_stale_reviews_on_push)

required_status_checks.description: contexts to require; empty emits no rule

admin_bypass.description: add the repo Admin role to bypass_actors

merge.description: the repo merge-button policy (squash-only, auto-delete head branch, allow-auto-merge) via PATCH /repos.

squash_merge.description: allow_squash_merge (the only merge model by default)

delete_branch_on_merge.description: auto-delete the head branch on merge

allow_auto_merge.description: allow a PR to auto-merge when its gate is green

ghas.description: GitHub Advanced Security: dependency graph + vuln-alerts + Dependabot + secret-scanning + CodeQL.

actions.description: GitHub Actions permissions: enabled + allowed_actions + the default GITHUB_TOKEN scope.

browser.description: settings the REST API does NOT expose, driven via agent-browser (gated off at apply unless RIG_GH_BROWSER=1).

enabled.description: plan the agent-browser backend (status lists it)

tmux.description: rig-managed tmux configuration (persistence across reboots, plugin-init ordering).

conf_path.description: the user's tmux config rig migrates

cc_restore.description: per-window Claude Code resume by session id.

boot.description: a launchd agent that brings tmux up after a reboot.

autosave.description: an INDEPENDENT launchd saver (decoupled from continuum's status-right hook); when on, continuum's own autosave is disabled so there is one authoritative saver.

enabled.description: provision the independent tmux-autosave launchd agent (StartInterval = continuum.save_interval minutes)

stale_after.description: minutes: rig doctor/status flags a mature live server whose newest save is older than this

pane_titles.description: pane-border-status titles (position + format), and separately dropping tmux's default clock+date from status-right.

format.description: pane-border-format value (no date/time token by default; must not contain '"', '\\', '$', '#(' (a shell-exec token), or a non-printable character other than a plain tab)

clear_status_right.description: when `enabled` is on, also clear tmux's default clock+date status-right; a SEPARATE toggle (nested under `enabled`) so status-right can be left alone while keeping the border title

gitignore.description: a rig-managed block in git's global core.excludesfile (ignores harness worktrees machine-wide).

entries.description: the ignored paths inside the managed block

excludesfile.description: force a specific file instead of honoring core.excludesfile

spotlight.description: macOS Spotlight-exclude: drop .metadata_never_index into dependency/build dirs + a launchd re-sweep agent.

enabled.description: provision the Spotlight-exclude sweep + agent (opt-in, macOS)

deny.description: dependency/build dir basenames to exclude (REPLACES the default set)

extra.description: extra dir basenames to exclude (ADDS to the default set)

tools.description: the personal CLI tool ecosystem (tg/review/task/draw/…) rig installs + advertises at apply, by running each tool's own install.sh. rig also keeps a provisioned checkout FRESH: if a tool repo ships scripts/deploy.sh, apply runs it (ff-only git pull) even when already installed — opt-in per tool, non-fatal. Default OFF (opt-in): list tools under items. A per-MACHINE concern — belongs in the GLOBAL layer, not a committed rig.yaml.

target.description: managed PATH dir each tool symlinks its bin into

items.description: per-tool spec keyed by command name (repo / bin_dir / enabled)

additionalProperties.description: one declared tool: the repo whose install.sh rig runs, plus optional overrides.

repo.description: the tool's checkout dir (holds install.sh); default ~/xp/<name>-cli

bin_dir.description: override the managed PATH dir for this one tool

tg_ctl.description: the tg-ctl inbound daemon auto-started as a per-machine boot LaunchAgent (macOS).

ship_delegator.description: a per-repo .claude/scripts/pr-ship.sh delegator so `gh ship` works in this repo (ignored in .git/info/exclude).

linters.description: per-repo linter + formatter config files rig provisions/reconciles (tool + content per repo).

enabled.description: provision the declared linter/formatter config files

items.description: the config files keyed by a label; each is `{ tool, role, path, content, enabled }` (e.g. an `oxfmt` formatter writing `.oxfmtrc.jsonc`, a `ruff` linter writing `ruff.toml`).

additionalProperties.description: one linter/formatter config file rig writes/reconciles.

tool.description: the tool name (informational; drives the status/log label)

path.description: repo-relative path of the config file (no leading '/' or '..')

project_tools.description: repo-local integration carriers for Haft, Serena, and Sverklo.

haft.description: Haft FPF/spec carriers plus optional Codex MCP registration.

project_name.description: Haft project name; defaults to the repo directory name

project_id.description: stable Haft project id; generated from project_name when omitted

codex_mcp.description: merge the Haft MCP server into .codex/config.toml

require_decision.description: require explicit decisions for high-impact work

allow_autonomy.description: allow autonomous Haft execution by default

project_name.description: Serena project name; defaults to the repo directory name

languages.description: Serena language ids; auto-detected when omitted

read_only.description: disable Serena editing tools for this project

sverklo.description: Sverklo global registry/index integration for this repo.

register.description: register this repo in the global Sverklo registry

reindex.description: run sverklo reindex during apply (off by default)
